summary: Closed unmerged. The PR was opened as an exact-match attempt for `hsd_80394314`, but the only diff changed the linkage of the particle BSS symbol `hsd_804CF810` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.static.h`. The author closed it as a bad HERMES/local-runner draft after confirming the title/body target symbol did not match the actual changed symbol and that the decomp-dev bot's "No changes" verdict for `hsd_80394314` was correct.

key_files.role: only changed file; changed `hsd_804CF810` from `static struct ParticleScreenState` to non-static `struct ParticleScreenState`

what_changed: The single code diff removed `static` from the BSS declaration/definition at address comment `/* 4CF810 */`, changing `static struct ParticleScreenState hsd_804CF810;` to `struct ParticleScreenState hsd_804CF810;`.
No source change to the labeled target function `hsd_80394314` was present in the diff excerpt.
The PR body claimed an accepted exact match for `hsd_80394314` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c`, but the later author comment states this was a misrepresented HERMES draft.
smart_moves: The author closed the PR rather than asking reviewers to reason about a mislabeled or zero-progress diff.
The author explicitly recorded the failure mode: title-vs-diff symbol mismatch, leaked body-template variables, and zero-progress objdiff.
A pre-open soundness gate was reportedly added to the local runner to catch these failures before opening future PRs.
decomp_lessons: For automated match PRs, verify that the target symbol in the title/body is actually affected by the patch before review.
A header-only linkage/staticness change can influence BSS relocation/symbol matching, but it should not be submitted as a match for an unrelated function without objective objdiff progress.
Bot verdicts such as "No changes" against the labeled symbol are useful sanity checks when the diff touches only data declarations or unrelated symbols.
Generated PR bodies with unresolved template placeholders are evidence that the automation output needs validation before submission.
naming_conventions: Unidentified HSD symbols use address-derived names such as `hsd_80394314`, `hsd_804CF810`, `hsd_804CF7E8`, and `hsd_804D0908`.
Static header BSS entries are annotated with address comments like `/* 4CF810 */`.
Particle arrays in this area use descriptive camel-case names such as `psNumTexGroup`, `psFormGroupArray`, `psTexGroupArray`, and `psCmdListArray`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Do not count a build/verifier success as meaningful unless objdiff shows progress for the claimed target symbol.
Add a pre-open check comparing the claimed symbol against changed files/symbols to catch title-vs-diff mismatches.
Treat `.bss` relocation-symbol/static-linkage edits as highly targeted; confirm the relocation target is the symbol required for the function being matched.

review_feedback.feedback: Closed because HERMES labeled the PR for `hsd_80394314` while the diff only touched `hsd_804CF810`.
The decomp-dev bot's "No changes" verdict against the labeled symbol was correct.
Future local-runner PRs should be gated for title-vs-diff symbol mismatch, body template leaks, and zero-progress objdiff.

follow_up_queries: Find later accepted work that actually matches `hsd_80394314`.
Find later PRs touching `hsd_804CF810` or `ParticleScreenState` linkage in `particle.static.h`.
Search for HERMES pre-open soundness gate failures or fixes involving title-vs-diff symbol mismatch.
Check whether removing `static` from `hsd_804CF810` was later submitted in a correctly labeled particle/BSS relocation PR.
